story.iWant: phone-based JWT authentication strategy and guard
story.soThat: protected routes için kullanıcı kimliğini doğrulayabileyim
story.tasks: - Task 1: Install required dependencies (@nestjs/jwt, @nestjs/passport, passport, passport-jwt, @types/passport-jwt) - Task 2: Create auth module structure (src/modules/auth/ with subdirectories) - Task 3: Implement JWT Strategy with phone-based validation - Task 4: Create JWT payload interface and types - Task 5: Implement JwtAuthGuard with @Public() support - Task 6: Create @Public() decorator - Task 7: Create @CurrentUser() decorator - Task 8: Configure AuthModule with JWT and Passport - Task 9: Create test endpoints to verify JWT guard functionality - Task 10: Write unit tests for JWT strategy - Task 11: Write integration tests for guard and decorators

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. AC-2.1.1: `src/modules/auth/strategies/jwt.strategy.ts` implements PassportStrategy with phone-based JWT validation 2. AC-2.1.2: JwtAuthGuard extract & validate JWT from Authorization header 3. AC-2.1.3: Valid token → `request.user` populated with { sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles } 4. AC-2.1.4: Invalid/expired token → 401 Unauthorized 5. AC-2.1.5: `@Public()` decorator bypasses JwtAuthGuard 6. AC-2.1.6: `@CurrentUser()` decorator extracts user payload from request

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management (Phone-based)
doc.snippet: AC-2.1.1 through AC-2.1.6 define complete JWT authentication infrastructure. JwtStrategy implements PassportStrategy with phone-based validation, extracting JWT from Authorization header. Valid tokens populate request.user with { sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles }. Invalid/expired tokens return 401 Unauthorized. @Public() decorator bypasses JwtAuthGuard, @CurrentUser() extracts user payload.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: JwtStrategy: Passport strategy for JWT token validation. Inputs: JWT token from header. Outputs: Validated user payload. Owner: modules/auth/strategies/jwt.strategy.ts. JwtAuthGuard: Route protection guard implementation. Inputs: HTTP request. Outputs: Allow/deny decision. Owner: modules/auth/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - JWT Payload Interface
doc.snippet: JwtPayload interface: { sub: string (User ID), phoneNumber: string (unique identifier), domainID: string (multi-tenancy), roles: string[] (user roles), iat: number (issued at), exp: number (expiration) }. Phone-based authentication uses phoneNumber instead of email as primary identifier.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: JWT security: HS256 signing algorithm, JWT_SECRET minimum 32 characters (validated in Epic 1), token expiration 15-60 minutes configurable, stateless validation without database hit per request. User lookup in validate() ensures deleted users cannot authenticate.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: JWT validation performance: ~2-5ms crypto signature verification, ~5ms database user lookup (indexed by ID), total overhead per protected request < 10ms. No caching needed for MVP.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: NPM packages: @nestjs/jwt ^10.2.0 (JWT generation/validation), @nestjs/passport ^10.0.3 (Passport.js integration), passport ^0.7.0 (authentication middleware), passport-jwt ^4.0.1 (JWT strategy), @types/passport-jwt ^4.0.1 (TypeScript types). Epic 1 integration: PrismaService for user lookup, ConfigModule for JWT_SECRET access.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: Auth module structure: src/modules/auth/ contains auth.controller.ts, auth.service.ts, auth.module.ts, strategies/jwt.strategy.ts, guards/ (empty, guard in common/), decorators/public.decorator.ts, decorators/current-user.decorator.ts, interfaces/jwt-payload.interface.ts. Common guards: src/common/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts (reusable across modules).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - JWT Token Validation Flow
doc.snippet: Client → Controller: GET /api/protected with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> → JwtAuthGuard extracts JWT → JwtStrategy validates signature (JWT_SECRET) → Check exp → PrismaService user lookup → Attach user to request (req.user = payload) → Controller accesses via @CurrentUser() decorator → Return protected data. Invalid/expired tokens return 401 Unauthorized.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Authentication & Session Management
doc.snippet: JWT payload contains phoneNumber instead of email as unique identifier. All auth endpoints accept phoneNumber as primary input. Implementation: User.phoneNumber field is unique constraint, JWT validation uses phoneNumber for user identification.

artifact.reason: PrismaService will be injected into JwtStrategy for user lookup during token validation. Extends PrismaClient, implements OnModuleInit/OnModuleDestroy lifecycle hooks. Used to query User entity by ID to verify user still exists and not deleted.

JWT configuration module using registerAs pattern. Exports JWT_SECRET (min 32 chars validated), JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION (default '15m'), JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION (default '7d'). Will be accessed by JwtStrategy and JwtModule configuration via ConfigService.

User entity schema defines fields needed for JWT payload: id (UUID, sub field), email (currently unique, will add phoneNumber in Story 2.2), domainID (multi-tenancy), passwordHash, isActive, deletedAt (soft delete check in JwtStrategy.validate). RefreshToken and OTPVerification relations defined for future stories.
RefreshToken entity for token refresh mechanism (Story 2.4). Stores UUID token, expiresAt, userID foreign key with CASCADE delete. Indexed on token, userID, expiresAt for fast lookups.
OTPVerification entity for phone verification (Stories 2.2, 2.3). Stores 6-digit code, type (EMAIL/SMS), expiresAt, attempts (max 3), verified flag. Used in registration and OTP login flows.

story-context.constraints: - JWT_SECRET must be minimum 32 characters (validated in Epic 1 config, Story 1.7) - Token expiration configurable: access token 15-60 min (default 15m), refresh token 7-30 days (default 7d) - Stateless JWT validation: no database hit per request except initial user lookup in JwtStrategy.validate() - User lookup in validate() ensures deleted users (deletedAt !== null) cannot authenticate (return null → 401) - JwtAuthGuard location: src/common/guards/ (shared across modules), not src/modules/auth/guards/ - JwtStrategy location: src/modules/auth/strategies/ (auth module specific) - Decorators location: src/modules/auth/decorators/ (public.decorator.ts, current-user.decorator.ts) - Phone-based authentication: JWT payload must include phoneNumber field (ADR-002) - PassportStrategy extension pattern: extend PassportStrategy(Strategy, 'jwt') - AuthGuard('jwt') extension pattern for guard implementation - Reflector service for metadata-based guard bypass (@Public() decorator) - createParamDecorator pattern for @CurrentUser() extraction - ConfigService injection for JWT_SECRET access in JwtStrategy and JwtModule - PrismaService injection for user lookup in JwtStrategy.validate() - All routes protected by default when using @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), except @Public() decorated routes - Invalid/expired tokens must return 401 Unauthorized (standard HTTP error) - Performance target: JWT validation overhead < 10ms per protected request - Testing: Unit tests for JwtStrategy.validate() with mocked services, integration tests for guard with real HTTP requests

interface.name: JwtPayload
interface.signature: export interface JwtPayload { sub: string; // User ID (UUID) phoneNumber: string; // Phone number (unique identifier, E.164: +90XXXXXXXXXX) domainID: string; // Multi-tenancy support (UUID) roles: string[]; // User roles: ['admin', 'staff', 'manager'] iat: number; // Issued at (Unix timestamp) exp: number; // Expiration (Unix timestamp) }

interface.name: JwtStrategy.validate()
interface.description: Validates JWT payload and user existence. Returns payload if valid (user exists, not deleted), returns null if invalid (triggers 401 Unauthorized). Called automatically by Passport after JWT signature verification.

interface.name: JwtAuthGuard.canActivate()
interface.description: Checks @Public() metadata using Reflector. If public, returns true (bypass). Else calls super.canActivate() for JWT validation via JwtStrategy. Returns true if valid, throws UnauthorizedException if invalid.

interface.name: @Public()
interface.description: Metadata decorator to mark routes as public (no authentication required). Used with JwtAuthGuard to bypass JWT validation.

interface.name: @CurrentUser()
interface.description: Parameter decorator to extract authenticated user payload from request.user (populated by JwtStrategy.validate()). Returns JwtPayload with sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles.

interface.name: PrismaService.user.findUnique()
interface.signature: prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id: string } }): Promise<User | null>
interface.description: Prisma query to find user by ID. Used in JwtStrategy.validate() to verify user exists and not deleted (deletedAt check). Returns User entity or null if not found.

interface.name: ConfigService.get()
interface.description: Access JWT configuration values validated in Epic 1 Story 1.7. JWT_SECRET minimum 32 characters, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION default '15m'. Used in JwtStrategy constructor and JwtModule configuration.

tests.standards: NestJS testing framework with Jest. Unit tests use mocked services (PrismaService, ConfigService) to test business logic in isolation. Integration tests use @nestjs/testing TestingModule with supertest for HTTP endpoint testing. Test files: *.spec.ts for unit tests, *.e2e-spec.ts for integration tests. Coverage target: 80%+ for critical authentication paths. Test data: Mock JWT payloads, mock users with/without deletedAt, mock config values.
tests.locations: - src/**/*.spec.ts (unit tests co-located with source) - test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts (integration/E2E tests) - Coverage reports: coverage/ directory

ideas.test: Unit test JwtStrategy.validate(): - Test with valid payload and existing user → returns payload - Test with valid payload but non-existent user → returns null - Test with valid payload but deleted user (deletedAt !== null) → returns null - Test payload structure (all fields: sub, phoneNumber, domainID, roles, iat, exp)

Integration test JwtAuthGuard: - Test protected endpoint without token → 401 Unauthorized - Test protected endpoint with valid token → 200 OK, request.user populated - Test protected endpoint with expired token → 401 Unauthorized - Test protected endpoint with invalid signature → 401 Unauthorized - Test protected endpoint with malformed token → 401 Unauthorized
Integration test @Public() decorator: - Test public endpoint without token → 200 OK (no authentication required) - Test public endpoint with invalid token → 200 OK (bypass guard)

Unit test @CurrentUser() decorator: - Mock ExecutionContext with request.user payload - Verify decorator extracts and returns JwtPayload - Test with missing request.user → returns undefined (handled by guard)

Integration test AuthModule configuration: - Verify PassportModule registered with defaultStrategy 'jwt' - Verify JwtModule configured with JWT_SECRET and signOptions - Verify JwtStrategy provider registered and exported - Test module imports and exports
